Three documented routes: (1) skip `hom_sum` entirely — open `∘ₗ` via `LinearMap.coe_comp` first, then `LinearMap.sum_apply` distributes the inner LinearMap sum directly; (2) build the AddMonoidHom `(M ⟶ N) →+ (M →ₗ[k] N)` (the `hom`-projection itself) and call `map_sum` on it manually; (3) restructure the summand by introducing `let f := fun i => ...; show ... f.hom` so the discriminator tree sees `?f i` with `f` a closed-over let-binder. After step (b) lands, re-attempt the iter-091 chain (c)+(d-body)+(e)+(f)+(g)+closure with per-step LSP validation now that LSP works.
